message:
fix(fr-runtime): ACL DRYRUN validates command before ACL gate; clearselectors accepted [bd-frankenredis-aclrun, aclclrsel]

Three ACL parser/dispatcher divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

- ACL DRYRUN now validates the command exists (via
  fr_command::is_known_command) and has correct arity (via
  check_command_arity) BEFORE running the ACL permission check.
  Upstream acl.c::aclCommand DRYRUN does the same. Returns
  'ERR Command <CMD> not found' / 'ERR wrong number of
  arguments for <cmd> command' instead of the misleading
  permission-denied message.

- ACL SETUSER alice clearselectors now accepted as a no-op
  rather than rejected with 'Syntax error'. fr's ACL doesn't
  model selector blocks yet, so the keyword has no observable
  effect — but accepting it lets ACL config files / replication
  payloads load without aborting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
